edge_policy.description: Clusters are built from an evidence-protected reconstruction graph. Raw graph edges remain available for audit, but weak public-infrastructure or boundary edges do not merge accounts into one case cluster.
rules: Always keep edges that support a fired detector finding.
Keep low-degree non-account endpoints so mule UPI/cash-out destinations are not dropped.
Do not let high-degree public endpoints merge case clusters unless a fired detector already uses that edge.

cluster_summaries.summary: cluster_001 contains 1 connected account(s) and 767 transaction edge(s). The cluster evidence includes no scored pattern.

The data shows two connected bank accounts, acct_001 and acct_002, with a total of 619 transactions between them. These accounts share UPI identifiers, as indicated by pattern ID 16. The evidence group contains two transaction IDs, one from each account. The accounts are part of a cluster with a total score of 0.0.
Account acct_004 was evaluated as an isolated account with no established transactional link to other accounts in this case. It was associated with failed_reversed_transaction_detection.

case_summary: The case contains 3 account cluster(s), with 0 account(s) ranked for suspicious activity. The highest-priority cluster is cluster_003 with total score 0.00.
